questions.question: What logical operation does this puzzle represent, and when does it output T vs F?
questions.answer: This is an AND gate. It outputs T (interceptor T) only when BOTH bits A and B start pointed to the right. Any other combination (A right/B left, A left/B right, or both left) outputs F (interceptor F).

questions.question: What are the four possible starting states of bits A and B, and which interceptor does each case route the ball to?
questions.answer: 1) A=right, B=right → interceptor T. 2) A=right, B=left → interceptor F. 3) A=left, B=right → interceptor F. 4) A=left, B=left → interceptor F.

questions.question: Why is this considered a 'non-destructive read' puzzle?
questions.answer: The bits' states are read to determine the routing, but the puzzle doesn't require the balls to flip or change the bits - it only uses their initial states to make a routing decision.
